1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: READ IN THE name of your Lord who created,

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Read with the name of your Lord Who created,

1.author: Arberry
1.text: Recite: In the Name of thy Lord who created,

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: Recite thou in the name of thy Lord Who hath created -

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Read! In the Name of your Lord, Who has created (all that exists),

1.author: Itani
1.text: Read: In the Name of your Lord who created.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: Recite in the name of your Lord Who created,

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: Read! In the Name of your Lord Who created.

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: Read: In the name of thy Lord Who createth,

1.author: Qarai
1.text: Read in the Name of your Lord who created;

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: Read (Prophet Muhammad) in the Name of your Lord who created,

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: Recite in the name of your Lord who created -

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: (Muhammad), read in the name of your Lord who created (all things).

1.author: Shakir
1.text: Read in the name of your Lord Who created.

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: Read! In the name of your Lord, who created:

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: Proclaim! (or read!) in the name of thy Lord and Cherisher, Who created-

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: Hath created man from a clot!

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: Has created man from a clot (a piece of thick coagulated blood).

2.author: Maududi
2.text: created man from a clot of congealed blood.

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: He has created man from a clot.

2.author: Qarai
2.text: created man from a clinging mass.

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: created the human from a (blood) clot.

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: Created man from a clinging substance.

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: He created man from a clot of blood.

2.author: Shakir
2.text: He created man from a clot.

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: created man from a clot [of blood].

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: Created man, out of a (mere) clot of congealed blood:

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: Read, for your Lord is most beneficent,

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: Read, and your Lord only is the Most Beneficent,

3.author: Arberry
3.text: Recite: And thy Lord is the Most Generous,

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: Recite thou: And thy Lord is the Most Bounteous,

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: Read! And your Lord is the Most Generous,

3.author: Itani
3.text: Read: And your Lord is the Most Generous.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: Recite: and your Lord is Most Generous,

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: Read! And your Lord is the Most Generous.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: Read: And thy Lord is the Most Bounteous,

3.author: Qarai
3.text: Read, and your Lord is the most generous,

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: Read! Your Lord is the Most Generous,

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: Recite, and your Lord is the most Generous -

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: Recite! Your Lord is the most Honorable One,

3.author: Shakir
3.text: Read and your Lord is Most Honorable,

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: Read! Your Lord is the Most Bountiful One

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: Proclaim! And thy Lord is Most Bountiful,-

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: The One Who taught to write with the pen.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: Who hath taught mankind by the pen -

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: Who has taught (the writing) by the pen [the first person to write was Prophet Idrees (Enoch)].

4.author: Itani
4.text: He who taught by the pen.

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: Who has taught by the pen.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: who, by the pen, taught the human being:

4.author: Shakir
4.text: Who taught (to write) with the pen

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: He Who taught (the use of) the pen,-

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: Taught man what he did not know.

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: The One Who taught man all what he did not know.

5.author: Arberry
5.text: taught Man that he knew not.

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: Hath taught man that which he knew not.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: Has taught man that which he knew not.

5.author: Itani
5.text: Taught man what he never knew.

5.author: Maududi
5.text: taught man what he did not know.

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: He has taught man that which he knew not.

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: Teacheth man that which he knew not.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: taught man what he did not know.

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: taught the human what he did not know.

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: Taught man that which he knew not.

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: He taught the human being what he did not know.

5.author: Shakir
5.text: Taught man what he knew not.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: taught man what he did not know.

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: Taught man that which he knew not.

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: And yet, but yet man is rebellious,

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: Yes indeed, man is surely rebellious.

6.author: Arberry
6.text: No indeed; surely Man waxes insolent,

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: By no means: Verily man exorbitateth.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: Nay! Verily, man does transgress all bounds (in disbelief and evil deed, etc.).

6.author: Itani
6.text: In fact, man oversteps all bounds.

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: Nay, but verily man is rebellious

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: Indeed, surely the human is very insolent

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: Despite this, the human being still tends to rebel

6.author: Shakir
6.text: Nay! man is most surely inordinate,

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: Nay, but man doth transgress all bounds,

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: For he thinks he is sufficient in himself.

7.author: Maududi
7.text: for he believes himself to be self-sufficient.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: when he considers himself without need.

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: because he thinks that he is independent.

7.author: Shakir
7.text: Because he sees himself free from want.

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: In that he looketh upon himself as self-sufficient.

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: Surely your returning is to your Lord.

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: Indeed towards your Lord only is the return.

8.author: Arberry
8.text: Surely unto thy Lord is the Returning.

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Verily Unto thy Lord is the return.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: Surely! Unto your Lord is the return.

8.author: Itani
8.text: But to your Lord is the return.

8.author: Maududi
8.text: Surely to your Lord is your return.

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: Surely, unto your Lord is the return.

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: Lo! unto thy Lord is the return.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: Indeed to your Lord is the return.

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: Indeed, to your Lord is the returning.

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: Indeed, to your Lord is the return.

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: However, (all things) will return to your Lord.

8.author: Shakir
8.text: Surely to your Lord is the return.

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: truly, all will return to your Lord.

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: Verily, to thy Lord is the return (of all).

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: Have you seen him who restrains

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: What is your opinion – regarding him who forbids –

9.author: Arberry
9.text: What thinkest thou? He who forbids

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: Have you (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) seen him (i.e. Abu Jahl) who prevents,

9.author: Itani
9.text: Have you seen him who prevents?

9.author: Maududi
9.text: Did you see him who forbids

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: Have you seen him who prevents

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: Hast thou seen him who dissuadeth

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: What do you think? Have you seen he who forbids

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: Have you seen the one who forbids

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: Have you seen the one who prohibits

9.author: Shakir
9.text: Have you seen him who forbids

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: Have you seen one who prevents

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: A votary when he turns to his devotions?

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: A bondman when he offers the prayer?

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: A bondman of Ours when he prayeth *

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: A slave (Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) when he prays?

10.author: Maududi
10.text: a servant (of Allah) when he prays?

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: a servant of Ours from prayer?

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: A votary when he (turns) to pray?

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: Have you thought, if he had been on guidance

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: What is your opinion – if he were upon guidance,

11.author: Arberry
11.text: What thinkest thou? If he were upon guidance

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Bethinkest thou, if he is himself guided,

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: Tell me, if he (Muhammad (Peace be upon him)) is on the guidance (of Allah)?

11.author: Itani
11.text: Do you think he is upon guidance?

11.author: Maududi
11.text: Did you consider: what if he is on the Right Way,

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: Have you seen if he is on the guidance

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Hast thou seen if he relieth on the guidance (of Allah)

11.author: Qarai
11.text: tell me, should he be on [true] guidance,

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: Have you seen if he was upon guidance

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: Have you seen if he is upon guidance

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: What will happen if the praying person is rightly guided

11.author: Shakir
11.text: Have you considered if he were on the right way,

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: Do you think he is rightly guided,

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: Seest thou if he is on (the road of) Guidance?-

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: Or had enjoined piety, (it would have been better)?

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: He would enjoin piety, so how good it would be!

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: or if he commands others to maintain piety!?

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: Have you thought that if he denies and turns away,

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: What is your opinion – if he denies and turns away, how wretched will be his state!

13.author: Arberry
13.text: What thinkest thou? If he cries lies, and turns away -

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: Bethinkest thou, if he belieth and turneth away?

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: Tell me if he (the disbeliever, Abu Jahl) denies (the truth, i.e. this Quran), and turns away?

13.author: Itani
13.text: Do you see how he disbelieved and turned away?

13.author: Maududi
13.text: Did you consider: what if he gives the lie (to the Truth) and turns away (from it)?

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: Have you seen if he denies and turns away

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: Hast thou seen if he denieth (Allah's guidance) and is froward?

13.author: Qarai
13.text: tell me, should he call him a liar and turn away

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: What do you think? Have you seen if he belies and turns away,

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: Have you seen if he denies and turns away -

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: What will happen if the prohibiting rejects the Truth and turns away from it!?

13.author: Shakir
13.text: Have you considered if he gives the lie to the truth and turns (his) back?

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: Do you see how he has denied the truth and turned away from it?

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: Seest thou if he denies (Truth) and turns away?

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: Does he not know that God sees?

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: Did he not realise that Allah is watching?

14.author: Arberry
14.text: Did he not know that God sees?

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: Knoweth he not that Allah beholdeth?

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: Knows he not that Allah does see (what he does)?

14.author: Itani
14.text: Does he not know that God sees?

14.author: Maududi
14.text: Does he not know that Allah sees everything?

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: Knows he not that Allah sees

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: Is he then unaware that Allah seeth?

14.author: Qarai
14.text: —does he not know that Allah sees [him]?

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: does he not know that Allah sees?

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: Does he not know that Allah sees?

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: Does he not realize that God sees him?

14.author: Shakir
14.text: Does he not know that Allah does see?

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: Does he not know that God observes all things?

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: Knoweth he not that Allah doth see?

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: And yet indeed if he does not desist We shall drag him by the forelock,

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: Yes certainly, if he does not desist, We will seize him by the forelock.

15.author: Arberry
15.text: No indeed; surely, if he gives not over, We shall seize him by the forelock,

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: By no means! If he desist not We shall seize and deal him by the forelock -

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: Nay! If he (Abu Jahl) ceases not, We will catch him by the forelock,

15.author: Itani
15.text: No. If he does not desist, We will drag him by the forelock.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: No indeed; if he does not desist, We shall drag him by the forelock;

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: Nay! If he ceases not, We will scorch his forehead --

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Nay, but if he cease not We will seize him by the forelock -

15.author: Qarai
15.text: No indeed! If he does not cease, We shall seize him by the forelock,

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: Indeed, if he does not desist, We will seize him by the forelock,

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: No! If he does not desist, We will surely drag him by the forelock -

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: Let him know that if he does not desist, We shall certainly drag him by his forelocks,

15.author: Shakir
15.text: Nay! if he desist not, We would certainly smite his forehead,

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: Let him beware! If he does not desist, We will drag him by the forelock --

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: Let him beware! If he desist not, We will drag him by the forelock,-

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Kalla lain lam yantahi lanasfaAAan bialnnasiyati

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: By the lying, the sinful forelock.

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: A forelock that lies, is sinful.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: by the lying forelock steeped in sin.

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: So let him call his associates,

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: So let him now call his gang!

17.author: Arberry
17.text: So let him call on his concourse!

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: Then, let him call his assembly,

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: Then, let him call upon his council (of helpers),

17.author: Itani
17.text: Let him call on his gang.

17.author: Maududi
17.text: So let him summon his helpmates;

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: Then let him call upon his council.

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: Then let him call upon his henchmen!

17.author: Qarai
17.text: Then let him call out his gang!

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: So, let him call upon his way!

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: Then let him call his associates;

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: Let him call on his associates for help

17.author: Shakir
17.text: Then let him summon his council,

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: Then let him call his associates;

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: Then, let him call (for help) to his council (of comrades):

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: We shall call the guards of Hell.

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: We will now call the guards!

18.author: Arberry
18.text: We shall call on the guards of Hell.

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: We also shall call the infernal guards.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: We will call the guards of Hell (to deal with him)!

18.author: Maududi
18.text: We too shall summon the guards of Hell.

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: We will call out the guards of Hell!

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: We will call the guards of hell.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: We [too] shall call the keepers of hell.

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: We, will call the Zabania (the harsh angels of Hell).

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: We will call the angels of Hell.

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: and We too will call the stern and angry keepers of hell.

18.author: Shakir
18.text: We too would summon the braves of the army.

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: We shall summon the guards of Hell.

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: We will call on the angels of punishment (to deal with him)!

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: Beware! Do not obey him, but bow in adoration and draw near (to your Lord).

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: Yes indeed; do not listen to him – and prostrate, and become close to Us. (Command of Prostration # 14)

19.author: Arberry
19.text: No indeed; do thou not obey him, and bow thyself, and draw nigh.

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: By no means! Obey not thou him. Continue to adore, and continue to draw nigh.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: Nay! (O Muhammad (Peace be upon him))! Do not obey him (Abu Jahl). Fall prostrate and draw near to Allah!

19.author: Itani
19.text: No, do not obey him; but kneel down, and come near.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: No, not at all. Never obey him. But prostrate yourself and become nigh (to your Lord).

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: Nay! Do not obey him. Fall prostrate and draw near (to Allah)!

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: Nay, Obey not thou him. But prostrate thyself, and draw near (unto Allah).

19.author: Qarai
19.text: No indeed! Do not obey him, but prostrate and draw near [to Allah]!

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: No, indeed; do not obey him! Prostrate and come nearer (to Allah).

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: No! Do not obey him. But prostrate and draw near [to Allah].

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: (Muhammad), never yield to him! Prostrate yourself and try to come closer to God.

19.author: Shakir
19.text: Nay! obey him not, and make obeisance and draw nigh (to Allah).

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: No indeed! Do not obey him, but prostrate yourself and come closer to God.

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: Nay, heed him not: But bow down in adoration, and bring thyself the closer (to Allah)!
